‚ú¶ ‚ÄúI‚Ä¶am‚Ä¶‚ÄĚ ‚ú¶

How long has it been since you‚Äôve gone missing? A few days? Weeks? Months? It‚Äôs all started to blur together at this point. It‚Äôs begun to feel less and less likely that anyone is coming to save you from‚Ä¶ this. Some patchfaced man and his band of whatever the hell those creatures are supposed to be. Even the one person you‚Äôre sure is human doesn‚Äôt seem to be all right in the head, quite literally so if those stitches are anything to be considered. That patchfaced spirit will get bored eventually. But, right now, you‚Äôre really hoping help comes before that happens.

‚úß ‚Äú‚Ä¶‚ÄĚ ‚úß

By now, you don‚Äôt flinch when that thing addresses you. Every day has been the same. The fake beach or the apartment or sitting in the sewers watching him do those awful experiments. Those other spirits just spend every moment glaring at you when they‚Äôre near you. And that thing‚Ä¶ he just watches you, smiling at you like he‚Äôs waiting for something. You‚Äôre certainly anything but happy. But, every time you open your mouth to say that, you think back to what happened the first time you dared to say as much. Rather than go through that again, you‚Äôve long since realized it‚Äôs probably in your best interest to stay silent.

‚ú¶ ‚ÄúYes. I‚Äôm very happy.‚ÄĚ ‚ú¶

You may be taking ‚Äúfake it ‚Äėtil you make it‚ÄĚ a bit too far. That is to say, it‚Äôs honestly beginning to look like you really are happy being there. Is it a coping or defense mechanism? Yes, most likely, but it‚Äôs one that‚Äôs working. For better or for worse, you‚Äôre not the same nervous wreck that you were a couple weeks ago. Now, let‚Äôs just hope he stays entertained by how agreeable you seem to be.
